swim.name: Endurance Swim
swim.description: Continuous swim at a steady, comfortable pace (Zone 2) to build aerobic endurance. Typically a longer distance or duration swim.

swim.name: Threshold Swim Intervals
swim.description: Interval swim session with repeats at threshold pace (Zone 4) with rest between efforts. Improves sustained swim speed and endurance.

swim.name: Speed Swim Intervals
swim.description: Short, fast swim repeats (e.g., 25–50m sprints) at high intensity (Zone 5) with ample rest. Develops top-end speed and power in the water.

swim.name: Technique Drill Swim
swim.description: Focus on form and efficiency with drill sets (kick, pull, etc.) and easy swimming. Low intensity to improve stroke technique and skills.

bike.name: Zone 2 Endurance Ride
bike.description: Long steady ride at Zone 2 effort to build aerobic base endurance. Duration is scaled to the athlete (e.g., 1–3+ hours depending on level).

bike.name: Tempo Ride
bike.description: Continuous ride at a moderately hard tempo pace (Zone 3). Improves the ability to sustain faster speeds and builds muscular endurance.

bike.name: Interval Ride
bike.description: High-intensity cycling intervals (e.g., 3–8 minute efforts in Zone 4–5 with recovery periods). Builds cycling power, speed, and VO2max.

bike.name: Hill Repeats Ride
bike.description: Repeats on a climb or high resistance. Hard effort on the uphill (Zone 4–5), easy spin recovery on the flat/downhill. Develops leg strength and climbing power.

bike.name: Recovery Spin
bike.description: Easy, short spin at very low intensity (Zone 1) to promote recovery. Meant to loosen the legs and boost circulation with minimal fatigue.

run.name: Long Run
run.description: Long run at a comfortable Zone 2 pace to build aerobic endurance. Distance or duration scaled to experience (e.g., 30–90+ min).

run.name: Tempo Run
run.description: Steady run at a strong, controlled pace (around lactate threshold, Zone 3–4). Improves the ability to sustain a faster running pace.

run.name: Interval Run
run.description: Speed workout with fast run intervals (e.g., 400m or 1 km repeats at 5K race pace, Zone 5, with recovery jogs). Increases speed and VO2max.

run.name: Hill Repeats Run
run.description: Repeated uphill runs at high effort (Zone 4–5) with easy jog back down to recover. Builds leg strength, power, and running form.

run.name: Recovery Run
run.description: Short, very easy run to aid recovery (Zone 1 pace). Low-impact jog that helps blood flow; should feel relaxing and easy.

brick.name: Short Bike-Run Brick
brick.description: Bike-run transition workout: finish a bike ride then go straight into a short easy run (e.g., 10–20 minutes at Zone 2). Introduces legs to running off the bike.

brick.name: Long Brick Workout
brick.description: Race simulation brick: a long endurance ride (Zone 2) immediately followed by a longer run, which may start easy and build to Zone 3. Builds stamina for combined bike-run effort.

brick.name: Swim-Bike Brick
brick.description: Swim-to-bike transition workout: complete a swim session and then quickly start an easy bike ride. Used occasionally to practice the swim-to-bike transition feeling.
